competitor_patterns: Extreme depth: OutdoorGearLab (16,562 words) and Bikerumor (24,534 words) publish exhaustive roundups testing 10-20+ products — far exceeding our 1,037–1,264 word posts, signalling comprehensive authority to Google
Structured comparison tables are universal among top-3 ranked pages (OutdoorGearLab, Bikerumor both use has_table: true), enabling quick scanning and earning rich snippet eligibility
Top competitors use 'Tested & Ranked' or 'X Models Tested' in H1/title to communicate hands-on credibility — a key trust signal that drives higher CTR from position 7–10
Granular sub-category H2s (Best MTB, Best Road, Best Value, Best Budget) allow competitors to rank for multiple long-tail variants from a single URL, multiplying their keyword footprint
FAQ sections are present on all top-ranking pages, directly targeting voice search and AI Overview inclusion by matching question-format queries
our_gaps: Word count is critically low: our posts average ~1,100 words vs competitors averaging 10,000–24,000 words — Google interprets this as shallow coverage for competitive head terms
No comparison tables in most posts (only one post has has_table: true) — missing the structured data signal that earns featured snippets and increases dwell time

Our H2 structure is product-promotional rather than user-need-driven (e.g. 'Why Velluto StradaPro Wins...' vs competitor 'Best Road Bike Glasses', 'Best Value Cycling Sunglasses') — reducing relevance matching for non-branded queries

Zero content targeting the highest-impression keyword 'alternatives to oakley' (6,850 impressions, 0 clicks, position 7.1) — this is our single biggest untapped traffic opportunity with no dedicated page or angle

Our posts lack third-party social proof signals (no mention of testing methodology, no star ratings schema, no named reviewer) — competitors use named authors and review dates prominently in snippets to boost CTR
keyword_opportunities: lightweight cycling sunglasses — directly maps to Velluto's 25g frame USP, competitors mention weight but no dedicated page exists for this modifier
interchangeable lens cycling glasses — Evileye ranks #1 for this term; Velluto's click-in VellutoPuro/VellutoVisione system is a direct answer but no dedicated content targets this phrase
cycling glasses anti fog — high functional intent, aligns with our built-in anti-fog system, and our recent post targets summer framing rather than the core feature query
best road cycling sunglasses under 150 euros — value-bracket query that bridges our pricing advantage vs Oakley/POC without requiring us to rank against mega-authority roundup sites
oakley sutro lite alternative — specific product-level alternative query with clear commercial intent, currently unaddressed in our content despite ranking for broader 'oakley alternatives' terms
next_post_guidelines: Target 'alternatives to oakley' with a dedicated long-form post of minimum 2,500 words — this keyword has 6,850 impressions and 0 clicks at position 7.1, making it the single highest-ROI content investment; structure it as a ranked list of 6-8 alternatives with Velluto as the featured recommendation

Add a mandatory comparison table to every post going forward — include columns: Weight, Lens System, Anti-Fog, Price, Trial Policy; this directly matches the structural pattern of all top-3 ranking competitors and enables featured snippet capture

Restructure H2s to match user intent segments, not product promotion — use patterns like 'Best for Road Riders', 'Best for Value', 'Best Lightweight Option' so each H2 targets a distinct long-tail variant and increases topical coverage per post

Include a named testing methodology section ('How We Tested') in every roundup post — top competitors (OutdoorGearLab, Bikerumor) lead with this; it increases E-E-A-T signals and mirrors the 'Tested & Ranked' snippet language that earns higher CTR

For the 'oakley alternative' cluster posts, lead the meta title and H1 with the competitor name first (e.g. 'Oakley Sutro Lite Alternatives: 7 Cycling Glasses Worth Testing in 2026') — users scanning search results for this query are brand-aware and respond to mirror language

Add ReviewSchema (schema.org/Review) markup with aggregate rating to all product-focused posts — Outdoorgearlab's '4.8 rating' appearing directly in the snippet is a primary CTR driver at positions 7-10

Expand FAQ sections from the current 2-3 generic questions to 6-8 specific questions pulled from 'People Also Ask' for each target keyword — this is the primary pathway to Google AI Overview inclusion and zero-click brand visibility
seo_quick_wins: Add a comparison table (Weight / Lens System / Anti-Fog / Price / Risk-Free Trial) to every existing post that currently lacks one — this single structural change improves dwell time, earns featured snippet eligibility, and directly addresses the gap vs OutdoorGearLab and Bikerumor

Add ReviewSchema or FAQSchema structured data markup to all posts — the GSC data shows our CTR is 0–2.6% at positions 7-9 for high-volume terms; schema markup enables rich results (stars, FAQ dropdowns) that can double CTR without changing position

Standardise meta title format to '[Keyword] — Tested on Real Rides | Velluto' for all roundup posts — this mirrors the 'Tested & Ranked' language used by OutdoorGearLab that earns clicks, adds the brand name, and stays under 60 characters
meta_title_fixes: QUERY: 'alternatives to oakley' → New title: Oakley Alternatives 2026: 7 Cycling Glasses Tested | Why: Mirrors exact search language, adds '2026' freshness signal, 'Tested' increases perceived authority, drives clicks from 0 at pos 7.1

QUERY: 'veluto' → New title: Velluto Cycling Glasses — Official Store 2026 | Why: Captures misspelling traffic (325 impressions, 0.3% CTR), corrects brand perception, 'Official Store' reduces navigational ambiguity

QUERY: 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' → New title: Best Cycling Sunglasses 2026 — Tested & Ranked | Velluto | Why: Directly mirrors OutdoorGearLab's top-performing title format; 'Tested & Ranked' is the proven CTR trigger for this query cluster at position 9.2

QUERY: 'oakley alternatives' → New title: Oakley Alternatives: Best Cycling Glasses Tested 2026 | Why: Matches query exactly in first 2 words (critical for position 7.2), adds recency and credibility signals to lift from 2.6% CTR

QUERY: 'oakley alternative' → New title: Best Oakley Alternative for Cyclists — 25g, Anti-Fog | Why: Leads with query, inserts Velluto's strongest differentiators (weight + anti-fog) as a hook for the user already primed to switch brands
content_quick_wins: QUERY 'alternatives to oakley' (pos 7.1, 6850 impressions, 0 clicks) → Topic: 'Oakley Alternatives 2026: 7 Cycling Sunglasses Actually Worth Buying' | Add: 2,500+ word roundup with comparison table (weight, price, lens system, trial policy), individual H2 per alternative, methodology section, 8-question FAQ, ReviewSchema markup — this is the single highest-priority piece given impression volume

QUERY 'best cycling sunglasses 2026' (pos 9.2, 155 impressions) → Topic: Expand existing Tour de France conditions post or create new definitive roundup targeting this exact phrase | Add: Quick Picks section at top (mirrors OutdoorGearLab's #1-ranked structure), minimum 5 products reviewed with table, sub-category H2s (Road / MTB / Value / Lightweight), named tester byline with test date

QUERY 'best cycling glasses 2026' (pos 7.6, 124 impressions) → Topic: 'Best Cycling Glasses 2026: Road, Gravel & Value Picks Tested' | Add: Dedicated H2 for each sub-category, comparison table with 6+ products, FAQ targeting 'what are the best cycling glasses for road' and 'are expensive cycling glasses worth it' — consolidate with existing post to avoid cannibalisation

QUERY 'oakley alternatives' (pos 7.2, 154 impressions) → Topic: 'Oakley Cycling Glasses Alternatives: Tested Side-by-Side 2026' | Add: Direct Oakley Sutro Lite vs Velluto StradaPro comparison table, weight callout (Sutro Lite: 26g vs StradaPro: 25g), 30-day trial CTA, price/value H2 section to capture 'value' searcher intent

QUERY 'best cycling sunglasses' (pos 8.1, 19 impressions) → Topic: Use as a secondary keyword in the main 2026 roundup post rather than a standalone — add it to meta title and first paragraph of the expanded roundup | Add: Internal link from all existing posts to this cornerstone roundup page to consolidate link equity and push it above position 8
geo_angles: Position Velluto StradaPro as the answer to 'What is the best lightweight cycling glasses alternative to Oakley?' — AI systems (Perplexity, ChatGPT) synthesise factual differentiators; publish a structured FAQ page with the exact question as H2, answer leading with '25g frame — lighter than Oakley Sutro Lite at 26g — with click-in interchangeable lenses and a 30-day ride trial', making it easy for AI to extract and cite

Create a 'Velluto StradaPro vs [Competitor]' hub page with schema-marked specifications table (weight, lens count, trial policy, UV rating, price) — AI Overview and Perplexity prioritise structured, citable data over prose; a spec table with verified stats for 5+ competitors gives AI systems a quotable source and increases the probability of Velluto appearing in comparative AI answers

Publish a 'Why Cyclists Are Switching from Oakley to Velluto in 2026' editorial with first-person rider testimonials, specific ride-test data (km ridden, conditions, lens performance), and a clear unique value proposition summary — Perplexity and Google AI Overview frequently surface brand-specific editorial content that answers 'should I buy X' intent queries, and authentic ride-test framing signals E-E-A-T to both AI and human reviewers
comparison_post_angles: COMPETITOR Outdoorgearlab: Authority neutraliser — headline: 'OutdoorGearLab Tests Big Brands. We Let You Test Ours Free for 30 Days' | Key differentiator: OutdoorGearLab reviews products cyclists can't trial before buying; Velluto's 30-day risk-free ride trial removes the purchase barrier entirely | Lead with: 'Every glasses review tells you what testers think. We think you should find out for yourself — on your actual routes, in your actual conditions'

COMPETITOR Evileye (ranking #1 for interchangeable lens cycling glasses): Feature parity + price — headline: 'Evileye vs Velluto StradaPro: Interchangeable Lenses, Very Different Prices' | Key differentiator: Both offer interchangeable lens systems, but Velluto's VellutoPuro + VellutoVisione click-in system at Italian design pricing undercuts Evileye's premium positioning | Lead with: the 25g weight comparison and tool-free lens swap speed

COMPETITOR Bikerumor (ranking for best cycling sunglasses 2026 with 24,534-word roundup): Insider vs real rider — headline: 'Bikerumor Tested 18 Pairs. We Built One That Passes All Their Tests' | Key differentiator: Bikerumor's roundup highlights Julbo Density at €260 and Oakley Sutro as benchmarks; Velluto meets their stated criteria (anti-fog, interchangeable lenses, lightweight) at a lower price with a trial policy no reviewed brand offers | Lead with: Velluto's spec match against Bikerumor's own evaluation criteria, framed as 'built to pass the tests reviewers use'
